shape.entryPoint: output/final/index.html (open via file:// in a real browser)
shape.vendor: output/final/vendor/leaflet/ (leaflet.js, leaflet.css, and any image assets the CSS references)
shape.modules: output/final/js/ (section-1, section-2, section-3 source files, loaded by index.html via <script> tags)
shape.styles: output/final/css/ (page-level CSS for header, layout, legend, error banner)
runtimeNetworkDependencies: OSM raster tile servers (required for the tile layer to render)

notes: section-4 receives the integrated artifact at output/final/. It opens index.html via file:// in a real browser engine. It must NOT substitute window.fetch, the L global, or the tile layer for the production-fidelity verification pass. Negative-path checks (e.g., the error banner) may be performed in a separate run with a deliberately bad feed URL — that is a configuration change to the artifact, not a runtime stub. The two listed runtime network dependencies are intrinsic to the prompt's spirit ('real earthquake activity', 'a map'); failure to reach either is a real failure mode the artifact must handle gracefully.
